runs.name: master_ingredients_loader_v2
runs.root_cause: cross-file terminal nodes rule applied (all GT DB nodes present) but agent created duplicate per-call-site nodes (db_execute1, db_execute2, db_connect1, db_connect2) instead of shared terminal nodes. Also included extra non-pipeline methods: setup_logging, _load_hierarchy, _validate_hierarchy, get_database(), get_placeholder(), db_util.table_exists(), db_util.rollback().
runs.fix_needed: Shared terminal node rule: create ONE terminal node per distinct cross-file method name; multiple callers point to it. Main pipeline only: exclude methods not in the primary call chain.

runs.name: cuisine_hierarchy_loader_v2
runs.root_cause: cross-file terminal nodes rule applied (db.execute, db.commit, db.fetch_one present) but included extra nodes: __init__, _load_data, generate_report (not in main pipeline), db.rollback (exception path excluded from GT). Missing GT edge: db.commit → _process_node (agent shows load_hierarchy → _process_node instead, skipping the chain through db.commit).
runs.fix_needed: Main pipeline only rule: exclude initialization chains (__init__, _load_data), utility methods (generate_report), and exception paths (rollback). Preserve the sequential chain structure (A→B→C→D) rather than flattening to common ancestor.

runs.name: sync_v2
runs.root_cause: Python-files-only rule correctly applied (no FastAPI Client or PostgreSQL DB actors). But agent failed to include sync.py's direct db.execute() and db.fetchall() calls as sync→pgutil messages. Agent treated 'db.execute()' as 'unresolved' because parser didn't create cross-file edges for instance method calls on a returned object (db = get_database() returns PostgresDatabaseUtility). Agent then showed library internals (factory→pgutil, pgutil→factory) violating no-library-traversal rule.
runs.fix_needed: Instance method tracking rule: when a local variable holds an instance from another Python file (e.g., db = get_database() returns PostgresDatabaseUtility from pg_database_utility.py), ALL method calls on that variable (db.execute, db.fetchall) are cross-file calls to that file. Show them as Actor→target: method() messages.

runs.name: local_recommender_v1
runs.notes: Perfect match. Both GT and skill correctly identified 3 classes with 0 structural edges (all fields are primitives or generic containers). classDiagram rules correctly applied.

runs.name: overlap_sync_manager_v1
runs.root_cause: DEGENERATE CASE: overlap_sync_manager.py uses only sqlite3 (stdlib) — no cross-file calls to other project Python files. Correct diagram has 1 actor and 0 messages. Skill invented a 'test_sync_manager' caller (hallucinated). Also: sequenceDiagram is the wrong diagram type for this file — classDiagram would be more appropriate.
runs.fix_needed: Degenerate case rule: if no cross-file Python file calls exist, produce diagram with ONLY the entry file as participant and 0 messages. Do not invent test harnesses or callers. Note: poor diagram type selection; sequenceDiagram requires cross-file call structure.

runs.name: reference_data_loader_v1
runs.failure_reason: hallucination=0.25 > 0.15 threshold (borderline fail — only 3 extra stdlib nodes)
runs.root_cause: Skill included json.load(), open(), Path.exists() as cross-file terminal nodes. GT correctly excluded these (file I/O is stdlib, not cross-file project DB utilities). The cross-file terminal nodes rule was over-applied to stdlib. Missing GT edge: get_cuisine_suggestions → _extract_all_cuisine_paths not captured by skill.
runs.fix_needed: Stdlib exclusion rule: json.load(), open(), Path.exists(), yaml.load() are NOT cross-file terminal nodes. Only project-internal DB utility calls and direct DB library calls (sqlite3, psycopg2) are terminal nodes.

runs.name: populate_reference_data_v1
runs.notes: PASS despite large size discrepancy (16 vs 45 nodes). Skill used shared terminal node pattern (1 sqlite3.connect() pointed to by all callers) vs GT's per-call-site pattern (6 separate sqlite3.connect() instances). Shared-node approach correctly covered 40/45 GT node concepts. json.load correctly excluded. All skill edges have GT equivalents. GT calibration note: GT had per-call-site duplication which inflates node/edge counts — shared node approach is more practical.

runs.name: recipe_entry_view_v1
runs.root_cause: Skill only read recipe_entry_view.py (2 classes: IngredientRowWidget, RecipeEntryView). GT included 5 additional classes from imported project files (AutocompleteEntryEnhanced, EnhancedAutocompletePopup, NumericEntry, YamlPreviewDialog, RecipeYamlConverter) and the 1 structural edge between imported classes. Skill prompt does not instruct reading imported local class files.
runs.fix_needed: Multi-file classDiagram rule: when primary file imports local project classes that appear as field types, read those imported files too. Include classes from imported project files in the classDiagram. This is how a complete class diagram is built across file boundaries.

v2_reruns.note: None of the 3 v2 re-runs passed. Cross-file terminal nodes rule was applied but over-elaboration (duplicate nodes, extra non-pipeline methods) caused high hallucination. sync.py v2 failed because agent did not trace db.execute/db.fetchall as cross-file calls to pg_database_utility.py.

new_gaps_identified.description: Use ONE shared terminal node per distinct cross-file method name. Don't create db_execute1, db_execute2, db_connect1, db_connect2 etc. Multiple callers can all point to the same terminal node.

Flowchart must show ONLY the primary execution pipeline. Exclude: initialization helpers not in main call chain (__init__ internals, _load_data), exception paths (rollback, error handlers unless central), utility report methods (generate_report), dead code.

When a local variable holds an instance returned from another Python file (e.g., db = get_database() → PostgresDatabaseUtility), ALL method calls on that variable (db.execute, db.fetchall) are cross-file messages to that file. Read source to identify the return type.
If entry point has NO cross-file calls to other project Python files (only stdlib), produce diagram with 1 actor (self) and 0 messages. Do not invent callers.
For classDiagram, when primary file imports local project classes that appear as field types, read those imported files too. Include classes from imported project files in the diagram.

json.load(), open(), Path.exists(), yaml.load(), csv.reader() are NOT cross-file terminal nodes. Only project-internal utility calls and direct DB library calls (sqlite3.connect, cursor.execute, conn.commit) are terminal nodes.
